update.currentPhase: Final leg of the outbound journey, entering the Moon's gravitational sphere of influence.
update.headline: Orion enters the Moon's Sphere of Influence as crew prepares for tomorrow's historic lunar flyby.
details: Spacecraft is on a stable free-return trajectory which will naturally swing Orion back toward Earth.
Crew successfully completed manual piloting demonstrations to test deep-space handling characteristics.
Mission Control is managing a minor urine venting issue caused by ice blockage using backup contingency bags.
Hardware failure reported on one crew laptop, though all critical mission systems remain unaffected.
update.crewStatus: Commander Wiseman, Glover, Koch, and Hansen are in good health and high spirits, performing exercise and suit checks.
update.lastEvent: Completion of deep-space manual piloting demonstration by mission specialists.
update.nextEvent: Lunar flyby with a planned 40-minute communications blackout.

milestones.label: Launch
milestones.detail: SLS liftoff from Pad 39B

milestones.label: Core Stage Separation
milestones.detail: MECO & core stage jettison

milestones.label: Solar Array Deploy
milestones.detail: Four Orion solar wings extended

milestones.label: Perigee Raise
milestones.detail: Orion performed perigee raise and apogee raise burns.

milestones.label: Proximity Ops
milestones.detail: Crew successfully demonstrated manual piloting of Orion near the ICPS upper stage.

milestones.label: Translunar Injection
milestones.detail: 5-minute, 50-second burn increased velocity by 1,274 fps to break Earth orbit.

milestones.label: ICPS Separation
milestones.detail: Upper stage jettisoned

milestones.label: AVATAR Scientific Payload Checkout
milestones.detail: Testing 'organ-on-a-chip' technology to study deep space radiation effects on bone marrow cells.

milestones.label: Earth Photography By Crew
milestones.detail: Crew captured images of Earth's auroras and zodiacal light.

milestones.label: Outbound Trajectory Correction (OTC-1)
milestones.detail: Maneuver skipped by mission controllers due to high trajectory accuracy.

milestones.label: Hygiene Bay Inspection
milestones.detail: Non-critical smell from waste management system identified as a dormant heater unit.

milestones.label: Lunar Distance Equalization
milestones.detail: Reached the point where Orion is exactly between the Earth and Moon.

milestones.label: CSA Live Downlink
milestones.detail: Live broadcast event featuring images of the lunar surface visible from the Orion spacecraft.

milestones.label: Deep Space Cpr Demo
milestones.detail: Crew practiced deep-space medical procedures including CPR and choking response.

milestones.label: Female Distance Record
milestones.detail: Christina Koch officially set the record for the farthest distance a woman has traveled from Earth.

milestones.label: Waste System Resolution
milestones.detail: Urine vent heater fix partially failed; crew transitioned to backup waste management.

milestones.label: Human Distance Record
milestones.detail: Orion officially surpassed the record set by the Apollo 13 crew in 1970.

milestones.label: Manual Pilot Demo
milestones.detail: Christina Koch and Jeremy Hansen tested Orion's manual thruster control in deep space.

milestones.label: Archer Study Participation
milestones.detail: Crew monitoring physiological responses to deep-space radiation via wearable sensors.

milestones.label: Lunar Sphere Of Influence
milestones.detail: Orion has transitioned into the Lunar Sphere of Influence where the Moon's gravity is the primary force.

milestones.label: Human Distance Record
milestones.detail: Expected to reach 252,799 miles from Earth, breaking the Apollo 13 record.

milestones.label: Lunar Surface Observation
milestones.detail: Crew completed a briefing for documenting specific craters and ancient lava flows on the lunar far side.

milestones.label: Lunar Flyby
milestones.detail: Planned pass 4,700 miles above the lunar far side; first humans to see it since 1972.

milestones.label: Communications Blackout
milestones.detail: Expected loss of signal as Orion passes behind the lunar far side.

milestones.label: Farthest Distance From Earth Record
milestones.detail: Orion surpasses the maximum distance from Earth of previous Apollo missions.

milestones.label: Earthrise Photo Ops
milestones.detail: Crew photographs Earthrise

milestones.label: ISS Crew Conversation
milestones.detail: Scheduled outreach event with crew members currently aboard the International Space Station.

milestones.label: Return Coast
milestones.detail: Free-return trajectory to Earth

milestones.label: Splashdown
milestones.detail: Final splashdown in the Pacific Ocean off the coast of San Diego.

milestones.label: Re-entry
milestones.detail: Skip re-entry at ~5,000°F

history.currentPhase: Final leg of the outbound journey, entering the Moon's gravitational sphere of influence.
history.headline: Orion enters the Moon's Sphere of Influence as crew prepares for tomorrow's historic lunar flyby.
details: Spacecraft is on a stable free-return trajectory which will naturally swing Orion back toward Earth.
Crew successfully completed manual piloting demonstrations to test deep-space handling characteristics.
Mission Control is managing a minor urine venting issue caused by ice blockage using backup contingency bags.
Hardware failure reported on one crew laptop, though all critical mission systems remain unaffected.
history.crewStatus: Commander Wiseman, Glover, Koch, and Hansen are in good health and high spirits, performing exercise and suit checks.
history.lastEvent: Completion of deep-space manual piloting demonstration by mission specialists.
history.nextEvent: Lunar flyby with a planned 40-minute communications blackout.

history.currentPhase: Deep space transit from Earth to the Moon
history.headline: Orion reaches lunar-gravity transition point as crew prepares for record-breaking flyby.
details: Spacecraft has passed the equidistant point and is now officially closer to the Moon than to Earth.
Engineers are managing a heater malfunction in the urine vent system; crew using backup contingency waste bags.
Mission Specialists completed manual piloting demonstrations to verify Orion's deep-space handling characteristics.
Medical drills including zero-G CPR and choking responses were successfully performed on Flight Day 4.
history.alertMessage: Urine vent system (lunar loo) heater fix only partially successful; secondary venting and backup bags in use.
history.crewStatus: Excellent health and high spirits; currently reviewing lunar science photography targets.
history.lastEvent: Manual piloting demonstration and zero-gravity medical emergency drill.
history.nextEvent: Lunar Flyby (Closest Approach) and record-breaking distance milestone on April 6.

history.headline: Artemis II crew surpasses halfway point to the Moon while managing minor technical issues with the waste system.
details: Orion has crossed the equidistance point and is now closer to the Moon than to Earth as it approaches the Lunar Sphere of Influence.
A recurring blockage in the urine vent has required the crew to use contingency collection bags while heaters attempt to clear suspected ice.
Mission specialists Koch and Hansen completed a 41-minute manual piloting demonstration to verify human-in-the-loop handling.
The crew performed zero-gravity medical drills, including CPR practice, to prepare for potential deep-space emergencies.
history.alertMessage: Recurring blockage in the Waste Management System urine vent; crew utilizing contingency procedures.
history.crewStatus: Crew is in excellent health and high spirits; conducting daily exercise and medical drills.
history.lastEvent: Manual piloting demonstration successfully completed by Christina Koch and Jeremy Hansen.
history.nextEvent: Surpassing the Apollo 13 all-time human distance record from Earth.

history.headline: Orion spacecraft Integrity is more than halfway to the Moon on precise free-return trajectory.
details: Mission controllers skipped the first planned trajectory correction maneuver due to highly accurate placement following the TLI burn.
A minor issue with the waste management system involving ice buildup was successfully resolved by Mission Control.
Crew members conducted key human-in-the-loop tests, including a 41-minute manual piloting demo and microgravity CPR drills.
Orion is approaching the all-time human distance record from Earth, with a lunar flyby scheduled for April 6.
history.crewStatus: Commander Reid Wiseman, Pilot Victor Glover, and Mission Specialists Christina Koch and Jeremy Hansen are in excellent health and high spirits.
history.lastEvent: Completion of manual piloting and microgravity medical response drills.
history.nextEvent: Entering the lunar sphere of influence and breaking the Apollo 13 human distance record.

history.headline: Orion Integrity navigates fourth mission day while troubleshooting waste system icing.
details: Ground teams are troubleshooting icing in the Universal Waste Management System (UWMS) urine vent line; crew is using backup urination devices.
Orion has passed the equidistance point and is now physically closer to the Moon than to Earth.
Mission Specialist Christina Koch and Jeremy Hansen successfully completed a 41-minute manual piloting demonstration.
One personal crew tablet has failed, though all mission-critical hardware remains fully operational.
sources: NASA Artemis II Mission Control Status Update
history.alertMessage: Recurring issue with the Universal Waste Management System (UWMS) involving icing in the urine vent line; contingency protocols in effect.
history.crewStatus: Crew remains in good health and high spirits; currently participating in the ARCHeR radiation study and medical demonstrations.
history.lastEvent: Completion of a six-degrees-of-freedom manual piloting test using Orion's thrusters.
history.nextEvent: Entry into the Lunar Sphere of Influence (SOI) where Moon gravity becomes the dominant force.

history.headline: Orion enters Moon's sphere of influence as crew prepares for historic lunar flyby.
details: Orion is transitioning from Earth-dominant to Moon-dominant gravity on Mission Day 5.
The crew successfully completed a 41-minute manual piloting demonstration to verify spacecraft handling.
Engineers are troubleshooting the Waste Management System due to a suspected ice blockage in vent lines.
Upcoming lunar flyby on April 6 will set a new record for the farthest distance humans have traveled from Earth.
history.alertMessage: Intermittent malfunctions reported with the 'Lunar Loo' waste system; crew is utilizing backup collection bags while a fix is evaluated.
history.crewStatus: Commander Wiseman, Pilot Glover, and Specialists Koch and Hansen are in excellent health and high spirits.
history.lastEvent: Successful 41-minute manual piloting demonstration completed by Koch and Hansen.
history.nextEvent: Lunar sphere of influence entry followed by closest lunar approach and Earth distance record.

history.headline: Artemis II crew enters Day 4 of translunar coast while troubleshooting urine venting system blockage.
details: A 41-minute manual piloting demonstration was successfully completed by Christina Koch and Jeremy Hansen to test deep space handling.
Mission Control cancelled the scheduled OTC-1 trajectory correction burn because the Orion spacecraft is on a near-perfect path.
Engineers are troubleshooting a suspected ice blockage in the urine venting system; crew is using backup CCU devices in the interim.
The crew is preparing for sleep after completing briefings on lunar photography targets for the upcoming far-side flyby.
sources: NASA Artemis II Mission Status Center
history.alertMessage: A blockage in the urine venting system, likely caused by ice, requires the crew to use backup collapsible urinal devices while ground teams troubleshoot.
history.crewStatus: Morale remains high; the crew is currently entering their sleep period following a productive day of science and manual thruster testing.
history.lastEvent: Completion of a manual piloting demonstration where specialists took control of Orion's thrusters for 41 minutes.
history.nextEvent: Entry into the Moon's gravitational sphere of influence.

history.headline: Orion spacecraft continues outbound transit as crew manages minor life support system maintenance.
details: The crew is performing manual flight handling tests and medical emergency drills.
A minor urine vent blockage is being addressed with temporary contingency measures.
The spacecraft is approximately 298,000 km from Earth and decelerating as expected.
The team is preparing for the historic lunar flyby and Earth-distance record-breaking event tomorrow.
sources: NASA Artemis II Mission Daily Report
history.alertMessage: Temporary contingency measures in effect for waste management system due to ice accumulation in urine vent.
history.crewStatus: Crew is in excellent health and high spirits while maintaining a rigorous exercise and test schedule.
history.lastEvent: Manual flight tests of Orion’s thrusters completed by Pilot Victor Glover.
history.nextEvent: Entry into the Moon's Sphere of Influence (SOI) expected later today.

history.currentPhase: Trans-Lunar cruising phase on a free-return trajectory.
history.headline: Artemis II breaks Apollo 13 human distance record as Orion cruises toward the Moon.
details: At 23:05 UTC, the Orion spacecraft Integrity surpassed the 248,655-mile distance record from Earth set by Apollo 13 in 1970.
Engineers successfully resolved an ice blockage in the Orion urine vent by using heaters and orienting the spacecraft toward the sun.
The crew remains in excellent health and has been conducting medical response drills and scientific checkouts of the AVATAR radiation payload.
The spacecraft is currently 406,773 km from Earth, traveling deeper into space than any previous human-rated vehicle.
sources: Artemis II Mission Management Team Update
history.crewStatus: Commander Wiseman, Pilot Glover, and Specialists Koch and Hansen are in excellent health and high spirits.
history.lastEvent: Surpassed the all-time human distance record from Earth at 23:05 UTC.
history.nextEvent: Lunar Flyby and communications blackout while passing the lunar far side.

history.headline: Artemis II passes halfway point to the Moon as crew resolves minor technical hurdles.
details: Orion has successfully crossed the equidistance mark between Earth and the Moon on Day 4.
A blockage in the wastewater vent nozzle caused by ice was cleared using heaters and solar orientation.
The crew performed a zero-gravity CPR demonstration and deep-space medical equipment testing.
Mission Control resolved a remote software issue with the crew's personal computing devices.
history.crewStatus: Excellent health and high spirits; focusing on space adaptation and lunar observations.
history.lastEvent: Successful restoration of the Orion waste management system functionality.
history.nextEvent: Entering the Lunar Sphere of Influence on Mission Day 5.

history.currentPhase: Deep space cruise toward lunar flyby
history.headline: Artemis II crew successfully passes the halfway point to the Moon while maintaining excellent health and resolving minor hardware issues.
details: Orion successfully resolved a clogged wastewater vent by rotating to expose the line to direct sunlight.
The crew exceeded 100 gigabytes of data transmission via the advanced optical communications system today.
Commander Wiseman and the crew are preparing for a manual piloting demonstration to verify deep-space handling characteristics.
Orion is on track to break the human distance record set by Apollo 13 on April 6.
history.crewStatus: Excellent; crew is in high spirits and adhering to the planned medical and operations schedule.
history.lastEvent: Hygiene bay toilet system maintenance and vent line thermal thaw.
history.nextEvent: Manual piloting demonstration by Pilot Victor Glover scheduled for 9:10 p.m. EDT.

history.currentPhase: Orion is cruising through deep space on a free-return trajectory toward the Moon.
history.headline: Artemis II crew passes mission halfway point while troubleshooting non-critical systems.
details: The spacecraft is on a highly accurate trajectory, allowing Mission Control to cancel the first scheduled Trajectory Correction Burn (OTC-1).
Crew members successfully performed a CPR demonstration in microgravity and other space adaptation exercises.
Engineers are troubleshooting a suspected frozen urine vent line; crew has transitioned to contingency waste bags.
A minor burning smell in the hygiene bay was identified as a non-critical heater or gasket issue and is not a safety concern.
history.crewStatus: Commander Reid Wiseman, Pilot Victor Glover, and Mission Specialists Christina Koch and Jeremy Hansen are in excellent health and high spirits.
history.lastEvent: Orion passed the halfway point by distance between the Earth and the Moon.
history.nextEvent: Manual piloting demonstration by Victor Glover to test Orion's deep-space handling qualities.

history.headline: The Orion spacecraft has successfully surpassed the halfway point to the Moon on its Artemis II mission.
details: Orion is currently on a precise free-return trajectory following an accurate TLI burn.
The crew is in excellent health and has successfully completed medical response drills.
Christina Koch has set a new record as the farthest woman to ever travel from Earth.
High-definition video and imagery are being successfully transmitted to Earth via the optical communications system.
history.crewStatus: All four crew members are reported to be in excellent health and high spirits.
history.lastEvent: Passing the halfway point between Earth and the Moon
history.nextEvent: Entrance into the lunar sphere of influence on April 5th

history.headline: Orion Integrity passes the halfway point to the Moon on a flawless trajectory.
details: Mission Control cancelled the first two outbound trajectory correction burns due to exceptionally precise navigation.
A minor heater odor in the waste management system was investigated and declared non-critical for flight operations.
The crew successfully completed microgravity medical drills, including a CPR demonstration and health kit checkouts.
Commander Wiseman captured high-resolution imagery of a crescent Earth as the spacecraft closes the remaining distance to the Moon.
history.crewStatus: Crew members Wiseman, Glover, Koch, and Hansen are in great spirits and reported feeling 'euphoric' while performing deep-space housekeeping and workouts.
history.lastEvent: Crossover point reached early on April 4; Orion is now closer to the Moon than to Earth.
history.nextEvent: Entry into the Lunar Sphere of Influence on April 5, followed by surpassing the Apollo 13 human distance record.

history.headline: Orion continues its journey toward the Moon, having officially crossed the halfway point of the outbound transit.
details: Mission Control canceled the second Outbound Trajectory Correction (OTC-2) burn due to the spacecraft's high-precision flight path.
The crew is managing a minor technical issue involving the Universal Waste Management System's heater, currently believed to be an ice blockage.
Crew members are performing medical experiments and CPR training in microgravity to support future Mars missions.
The spacecraft remains in excellent condition as it prepares for the upcoming lunar flyby.
history.crewStatus: Excellent health and spirits; the crew began Flight Day 4 today with a musical wake-up call.
history.lastEvent: Traversal of the halfway point between Earth and the Moon.
history.nextEvent: Surpassing the Apollo 13 human distance record on April 6, 2026.

history.headline: The Artemis II mission is successfully on a free-return trajectory and the crew has passed the equidistance point between Earth and the Moon.
details: Orion spacecraft is in excellent health with all primary systems performing within nominal parameters.
A minor odor reported in the hygiene bay was traced to insulation and is not a safety concern.
The crew crossed the halfway point of their journey on April 4 and are now closer to the Moon than Earth.
Flight controllers successfully canceled the first planned outbound trajectory correction due to the high precision of the TLI burn.
history.crewStatus: The four-person crew is in high spirits, in good physical condition, and maintaining a routine of cardiovascular exercise and medical training.
history.lastEvent: Crew crossed the halfway point of the mission journey on April 4, 2026.
history.nextEvent: Lunar Flyby on April 6, 2026.

history.headline: Artemis II Orion spacecraft reaches equidistance point between Earth and the Moon on Flight Day 4.
details: The first Outbound Trajectory Correction (OTC-1) burn was canceled because the spacecraft's path following TLI is exceptionally precise.
Mission Specialist Christina Koch officially became the farthest woman from Earth, surpassing the 70,000-mile mark.
The crew successfully addressed a brief 'burning smell' from the toilet heater insulation, which has been classified as a minor nuisance.
Astronauts are conducting medical demonstrations, including CPR in microgravity and health kit testing.
history.crewStatus: Commander Reid Wiseman, Pilot Victor Glover, and Mission Specialists Christina Koch and Jeremy Hansen are in high spirits and good health.
history.lastEvent: Reached the 'Equidistance Point' where Orion is now closer to the Moon than to Earth.
history.nextEvent: Lunar Flyby at an altitude of approximately 7,600 km from the surface.

history.headline: Orion spacecraft on precise trajectory to the Moon following high-precision TLI burn.
details: Mission controllers canceled the first trajectory correction burn (OTC-1) as Orion is maintaining a near-perfect course following the TLI burn.
The crew successfully managed a minor burning smell reported near the toilet hygiene bay, which was identified as non-hazardous insulation material.
Astronauts completed key medical and operational tests including a zero-gravity CPR demonstration and exercise equipment checkouts.
Orion has surpassed 245,000 km from Earth and is on track for a lunar flyby and record-breaking distance on April 6.
history.crewStatus: Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch, and Jeremy Hansen are in high spirits and excellent health; currently focusing on photography and lunar approach prep.
history.lastEvent: Translunar Injection (TLI) engine burn completed April 2.
history.nextEvent: Lunar gravity assist flyby passing within 4,700 miles of the lunar surface on April 6.

history.headline: Orion spacecraft Integrity has passed the equidistance point between Earth and the Moon on Flight Day 3.
details: Orion is now closer to the Moon than to Earth, following a precise Translunar Injection burn that resulted in the cancellation of the first trajectory correction maneuver.
A minor technical issue involving an odor in the waste management system on Flight Day 2 was successfully resolved by the crew and ground teams.
Mission Specialist Christina Koch has officially set a new record for the farthest distance traveled from Earth by a woman.
The crew successfully completed zero-gravity medical drills including CPR rehearsals and routine physical health monitoring.
history.crewStatus: Commander Wiseman, Pilot Glover, and Specialists Koch and Hansen are in excellent health and high spirits, settling into deep-space routines.
history.lastEvent: Reached the halfway point between Earth and the Moon (Lunar Distance Equalization).
history.nextEvent: Entry into the Lunar Sphere of Influence (SOI) and closest lunar approach.

history.headline: Orion passes the halfway point to the Moon as the crew completes critical medical and communications checks.
details: The spacecraft has crossed the equidistance point between Earth and the Moon, now more than 229,000 km from Earth.
Flight controllers canceled the OTC-1 trajectory correction burn due to the high precision of the SLS and ESM during TLI.
The crew performed microgravity CPR demonstrations and medical kit checks to test emergency protocols in deep space.
Christina Koch successfully verified the emergency communications system via the Deep Space Network.
history.crewStatus: Commander Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch, and Jeremy Hansen are in excellent health and great spirits.
history.lastEvent: Passed the lunar equidistance point (halfway mark) at approximately 04:00 UTC.
history.nextEvent: Entry into the Lunar Sphere of Influence (SOI) late Sunday followed by the lunar flyby on Monday.
